name: depth or fault
Farmers IoT Toolkit — Module 1 water level
info:
Receives readings from the ESP8266 water level node.

The ESP POSTs JSON like:

    {"node":"water-tank-1","ok":true,"raw":153,
     "depth_mm":127,"percent":47.6,"rssi":-62,"uptime_s":1234}

A failed sensor read still POSTs, with ok:false and an error
string — a silent node is indistinguishable from dead WiFi or a
flat battery, so the node says so instead.

IMPORTANT: the `http response` node is not optional. Without it
the request never completes and the ESP sits there until it times
out. Every `http in` needs one.

Test without any hardware:

    curl -X POST http://192.168.43.1:1880/water \
         -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
         -d '{"node":"test","ok":true,"depth_mm":500}'
